facts.text: India is the world's largest democracy and one of its most populous countries.
New Delhi is India's capital, while Mumbai is one of its biggest financial and entertainment centers.
The Taj Mahal in Agra is one of the most famous monuments in the world.
India is home to incredible linguistic diversity, with hundreds of languages spoken across the country.
Hindi and English are widely used at the national level, though states often use additional official languages.
The Ganges River holds deep cultural and religious importance in India.
India's climate is heavily influenced by the annual monsoon system.
Indian civilization includes ancient traditions linked to the Indus Valley, Vedic culture, and later empires such as the Maurya and Mughal.
Chess is believed to have originated in India in an early form called chaturanga.
Indian cuisine varies widely by region, featuring dishes such as dosa, biryani, curry, naan, chaat, and countless local specialties.
Bollywood, based in Mumbai, is one of the most prolific film industries in the world.
The Himalayas form India's northern frontier and include some of the world's most dramatic mountain landscapes.
India gained independence from British rule in 1947.
Modern India plays a major global role in technology, medicine, space research, business, and culture.
The Taj Mahal in Agra is one of India's best-known monuments and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Its white marble design helped make it one of the most famous buildings in the world.
Taj Mahal is one of the most recognizable places or topics associated with India.
Travel guides about India frequently feature Taj Mahal.
Learning about Taj Mahal helps explain part of the geography, culture, or history of India.
For many visitors, Taj Mahal stands out as a major highlight of India.
Maps, documentaries, and guidebooks about India often highlight Taj Mahal.
The story of Taj Mahal is closely tied to how many people understand India.
People around the world often associate India with places or traditions like Taj Mahal.
Taj Mahal is frequently mentioned when describing the diversity and appeal of India.
One reason India feels so memorable to travelers is the presence of places such as Taj Mahal.
From tourism to history, Taj Mahal plays a notable role in how India is presented to the world.
